insight: For Tunisia, the hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup have had a mixed impact on their momentum. On average, they tend to lose a bit of momentum after a break, with a mean change of -0.050 per minute, indicating a slight decline in their performance. However, the numbers also suggest that when Tunisia is not pressing before a break, they are able to regain some momentum afterwards, with an average change of +0.100 per minute, which could be a strategic advantage for them. In contrast, when they are pressing before a break, they tend to lose more momentum, with an average change of -0.200 per minute, which may indicate that they need to adjust their strategy to maintain their intensity after the break.
